issues.comment: The property `-ms-align-items` was never a valid vendor-prefixed CSS property in any version of Internet Explorer or Edge. The correct IE10 property for `align-items` is `-ms-flex-align` (which is already included on the line above). This line is dead code that all browsers will silently ignore. It suggests the author was guessing at the prefix pattern rather than using the actual IE10 flexbox implementation (`-ms-flex-align`).

The old flexbox spec (`-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group`) uses 1-based ordinal groups (default = 1), while the standard `order` property is 0-based (default = 0). The mixin passes the same raw value to both, creating an off-by-one mapping. The correct old-spec values should be `$val + 1` to match the standard `order` semantics. For example, `@include order(0)` would produce `-webkit-box-ordinal-group: 0` (invalid, values must be ≥ 1) and `@include order(1)` would be indistinguishable from the default ordinal group on old WebKit.
